_note: §A.8 #1 framing conformance vectors for the MCP-over-UDS shim wire. GENERATED from the real uds_frame.ts encodeFrame + FrameDecoder (NOT hand-computed). Feed input_hex (or chunks_hex in order) to a FRESH decoder per case and assert the outcome. A frame = u32 LE frameLength (counts the type byte + payload) | type byte | payload. type 0x01=MCP, 0x02=CTRL. Length must be >=1 and <= cap. Errors: FRAME_LENGTH_TOO_LARGE / FRAME_LENGTH_TOO_SMALL / FRAME_UNKNOWN_TYPE. Partial input buffers (0 frames, need-more) until the wire completes.

cases.name: valid_mcp_tools_list
cases.desc: MCP 0x01 frame, payload = a JSON-RPC tools/list request

cases.name: two_frames_one_chunk
cases.desc: CTRL then MCP concatenated; both decode from one push

cases.name: partial_then_complete
cases.desc: a valid MCP frame split mid-wire across two chunks; chunk1 buffers (need-more, 0 frames), chunk2 completes it

cases.name: oversize_length
cases.desc: header declares frameLength=16777217 (> 16777216 cap); rejected from the 4-byte header alone (no payload buffering)

cases.name: length_too_small_zero
cases.desc: header declares frameLength=0 (< 1; a frame must carry at least the type byte)

cases.name: unknown_type_byte
cases.desc: valid length=3 but type byte 0x03 (not 0x01 MCP / 0x02 CTRL); rejected as soon as the type byte is readable
